threat_model: PLang is user-sovereign software. The user owns their .pr files and runs them in a trusted environment. The trust boundary is cryptographic signatures on Data — once verified, everything inside runs with full trust. Security focuses on: (1) DoS at transport boundaries where untrusted data enters, (2) integrity of the signature/verification mechanism, (3) robustness against malformed external input.

attack_surface.exposure: UnwrapJsonElement (Data.cs:210) recursively unwraps JSON. fromJson.cs now delegates to Data.UnwrapJsonElement (deduplication complete).
attack_surface.trust_boundary: External data entering via transport channels, API responses, file reads from untrusted sources, decompressed envelope payloads
mitigations: Depth limit of 128 on UnwrapJsonElement recursion
Code deduplicated — single implementation in Data.cs
gaps: No size limit on JSON string parsing in JsonStringNavigator

attack_surface.trust_boundary: Decompress processes untrusted compressed+serialized Data from external channels/transport. This is the PRIMARY trust boundary.
mitigations: Verified is { get; private set; } — only SetVerified() can change it
gaps: No actual crypto in SetVerified() yet — method exists but verification logic is future work

attack_surface.trust_boundary: Navigation paths derived from external data (e.g., navigating into decompressed/deserialized structures from transport)
Type names from external sources (e.g., type field in deserialized Data from transport)
mitigations: Depth limit of 20 on Clr() generic parsing
Known type names are finite in _nameToClr

attack_surface.trust_boundary: Variable values that may originate from external data
mitigations: ThreadStatic HashSet cycle detection — circular references leave bracket unresolved instead of crashing

findings.vector: UnwrapJsonElement recursively unwraps JsonElement objects/arrays. Duplicate existed in fromJson.cs.
findings.preconditions: Untrusted JSON enters via transport channel, API response, or external file.
findings.resolution: Coder v5: Added depth parameter (max 128) to UnwrapJsonElement, UnwrapJsonObject, UnwrapJsonArray. fromJson.cs now delegates to Data.UnwrapJsonElement — duplication eliminated. Tests cover 200-level nested JSON.

findings.vector: RehydrateNestedData recursively reconstructs Data from dictionaries at the transport boundary.
findings.preconditions: Compressed payload with deeply nested {name,value} structures.
findings.impact: Process crash (StackOverflowException). DoS at transport boundary.
findings.resolution: Coder v5: Added depth parameter (max 128) to RehydrateNestedData. Tested.

findings.vector: Data.GetChild recursively parses dot/bracket paths without depth limit.
findings.preconditions: Deep path navigating into external data structures.
findings.resolution: Coder v5: Added depth parameter (max 100) to GetChild. Returns Data.FromError on depth exceeded. Tested in isolation. Coder v6: Fixed error reporting to return proper Data.FromError instead of null.

findings.vector: MemoryStack.Set() accepts writes to !-prefixed system variables.
ObjectNavigator exposes all public properties via reflection.
findings.impact: N/A — intentional capability in trusted environment.

findings.vector: JsonStringNavigator parses any string starting with { or [ as JSON with no size limit. Re-parses on every access.
findings.preconditions: Large JSON string from external source stored in variable, then navigated.
findings.impact: Memory exhaustion. Amplified by repeated access.
findings.proposed_fix: Add size limit before parsing (e.g., 10MB). Consider caching.
findings.resolution: Added 10MB size limit in CanNavigate — strings over 10MB are not treated as navigable JSON. Caching (parse once on first access) is a separate design improvement, not a security fix.

findings.vector: MemoryStack.ResolveVariablesInPath recursively resolves bracket variables. Circular references cause stack overflow.
findings.preconditions: Two variables reference each other in bracket notation.
findings.resolution: Coder v5: ThreadStatic HashSet cycle detection. Circular references leave bracket unresolved instead of crashing. Coder v7: Added regression tests using reflection to verify cycle guard fires and ThreadStatic cleanup works.

findings.vector: Newtonsoft compatibility shim detects JToken types by namespace string only.
findings.preconditions: Requires library.load (which already grants RCE) — redundant vector.
findings.impact: Low. Namespace spoofing adds nothing over existing RCE via library.load.

findings.vector: Verified was a public settable bool? with no cryptographic backing. Any code could claim data is verified without proof.
findings.preconditions: Any code path sets data.Verified = true.
findings.impact: Trust boundary bypass. Signatures are the wall — settable bool was a door anyone could open.
findings.proposed_fix: Make Verified { get; private set; }. Add SetVerified() for future crypto.
findings.resolution: Coder v5: Verified changed to { get; private set; }. SetVerified(bool) added as internal method for future crypto verification. External code can no longer claim verified status. Tested.

findings.vector: Clr() recursively parses generic type syntax without depth limit.
findings.preconditions: Deeply nested type name from external source.
findings.resolution: Coder v5: Added depth parameter (max 20) to Clr(). Coder v7: Added boundary tests — depth 20 resolves, depth 21 returns null.

findings.preconditions: Code path merging Data in a loop.
findings.proposed_fix: Add max size check on merged list.
Consider shallow clone or size limits.

summary: v3 — Updated after coder v5-v7 security hardening. 7 of 12 findings now fixed: all HIGH severity recursion DoS vectors (#1-3) have depth limits, Verified is private set (#9), cycle detection in variable resolution (#7), Clr() depth limit (#10), fromJson deduplication (#1). 2 findings by-design (#4, #5). 3 findings remain open: JsonStringNavigator size limit (#6, medium), Newtonsoft namespace check (#8, low — redundant vector), Data.Merge unbounded growth (#11, low), MemoryStack.Clone (#12, low). The critical trust boundary (Verified/Signature) and all transport-facing DoS vectors are now hardened. Remaining open findings are low-priority edge cases.
